From these, it is logically solvable closed-book that Strategy Y is more likely to suppress runaway because it interrupts the initiating reaction before the cascade, while Strategy X intervenes only after full runaway begins, when stopping it is not reliable; the positive electrode’s larger heat contribution is stage-dependent and does not imply it is the best prevention target. The gold answer’s claims are non-ambiguous given the stated reliability of pre-SEI negative-electrode cooling, and none of its required points contradict the evidence bundle.
